meta.title: Reaction Timer
meta.description: Create a simple reaction timer that detects when a button is pressed.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding digital input with pull-up resistors and basic event detection
meta.code_hint: Use digitalRead() on D2 to detect button presses and trigger a reaction timer

meta.title: Reaction Timer
meta.description: Create a simple reaction timer that detects when a button is pressed.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding digital input with pull-up resistors and basic event detection
meta.code_hint: Use digitalRead() on D2 to detect button presses and trigger a reaction timer

walkthrough.instruction: Place the button onto the breadboard across the center gap. Top legs in row 12, columns E and F. Bottom legs in row 14, columns E and F.
walkthrough.tip: The button bridges the two halves of the breadboard.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D2 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 14, column G to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
